items.briefing:
A vulnerability in Apple's Hide My Email feature has been discovered that could expose users' real email addresses — if your organisation uses Apple devices, staff should be aware their private emails may not be as hidden as expected. Separately, security experts are warning that organisations relying heavily on AI-driven security tools may be missing real threats, and that employee use of AI tools is increasingly happening without management oversight — both concerns relevant to Australian RTOs adopting new technology.

Phishing attacks are also becoming more sophisticated, automatically adjusting to the victim's device and operating system to appear more convincing. **Practical tip:** Remind staff never to click unexpected links or pop-ups, even if the page looks familiar and legitimate.

featured.title: SMS Sender ID Register goes live to help protect Australians from scams
featured.quote: New SMS register directly protects Australian consumers from scam calls and texts.
